name: Pinares de Fortanete y ascenso a la sierra de la Lastra — circular desde Fortanete (BTT03 Maestrazgo)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Fortanete offers diverse landscapes within the Maestrazgo region of Teruel, Spain. The village is situated at an altitude of 1353 meters, surrounded by robust mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Cañada and Tarrascón-Las Lastras, and extensive sessile pine forests. The terrain features high pastures, ravines, and significant elevation changes, providing varied conditions for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails in Fortanete The most popular mountain bike trail is Masadas y Casas de San Juan — circular desde Cantavieja (BTT06 Maestrazgo), a 20.3 miles (32.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through varied landscapes of the Maestrazgo. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pinares de Fortanete y ascenso a la sierra de la Lastra — circular desde Fortanete (BTT03 Maestrazgo), a moderate 31.3 miles (50.4 km) path. This route features pine forests and significant elevation gain as it ascends to the Sierra de la Lastra. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra de la Cañada and Capellanía Trail, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Cañada mountain range, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around Fortanete is defined by robust mountain ranges, extensive forests, and high pastures. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to very difficult. The routes in Fortanete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Fortanete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails around Fortanete?
A: The Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center, which has a reception point in Fortanete, offers over 100 km of marked trails catering to various skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy (green) to very difficult (black). While the mountainous terrain means many routes are challenging, like the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995595411/pinares-de-fortanete-y-ascenso-a-la-sierra-de-la-lastra-circular-desde-fortanete-btt03-maestrazgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Fortanete y ascenso a la sierra de la Lastra&lt;/a&gt;, the center is expanding its offerings to include easier options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Fortanete?
A: Mountain biking around Fortanete offers diverse landscapes. You'll encounter robust mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Cañada and Tarrascón-Las Lastras, extensive sessile pine forests, high pastures, and ravines. The routes often feature significant elevation changes, providing varied and engaging conditions for riders.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Fortanete area?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails available around Fortanete, with options for various ability levels. These include 2 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes, ensuring a wide range of experiences for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there facilities available for mountain bikers in Fortanete?
A: Yes, Fortanete is home to a reception point for the Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center, the first of its kind in Aragon. This center provides essential services including information, bike repair and rental, washing points, showers, and toilets.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Fortanete?
A: Yes, the Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center in Fortanete offers dedicated parking facilities for mountain bikers, making it convenient to start your ride.

Q: What natural features or landscapes can I see while mountain biking in Fortanete?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to ride through well-maintained sessile pine forests, high, cool pastures, and areas with dramatic ravines and mountains. In spring, the landscape is particularly vibrant with extensive fields of red poppies contrasting against green cereals. The area is also near the headwaters of the Pitarque River, a beautiful spot for nature observation.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Fortanete itself offers historical sights, including the baroque Church of the Purification, a 16th-century town hall, and various grand houses. You can also find the ruins of the 11th-12th century Castle of El Cid and several hermitages scattered throughout the landscape. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Pitarque River&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural attraction.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Fortanete?
A: Fortanete, situated at an altitude of 1353 meters, offers a pleasant climate for mountain biking. Spring is particularly beautiful, with extensive fields of red poppies. The high altitude means cooler temperatures, making it enjoyable during warmer months, but always be prepared for varied mountain weather.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails around Fortanete?
A: While many routes in the Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center are challenging due to the mountainous terrain, the center is actively expanding its offerings to include easier options suitable for families. It's best to check the specific trail descriptions for the most up-to-date information on family-friendly routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails near Fortanete?
A: The Maestrazgo region is generally welcoming to dogs on trails, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Please respect local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb the natural environment or other users.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Fortanete?
A: The mountain bike routes around Fortanete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the robust mountain ranges, and the extensive forests that define the area, providing a truly immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Fortanete are designed as circular trails. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995599051/masadas-y-casas-de-san-juan-circular-desde-cantavieja-btt06-maestrazgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masadas y Casas de San Juan&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995595411/pinares-de-fortanete-y-ascenso-a-la-sierra-de-la-lastra-circular-desde-fortanete-btt03-maestrazgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Fortanete y ascenso a la sierra de la Lastra&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: Can I find food and accommodation in Fortanete after my ride?
A: Fortanete village, with its monumental nucleus, offers options for food and accommodation. You'll find local cafes, restaurants, and guesthouses where you can relax and refuel after exploring the trails.
tips: The port of Villarroya separates the Sierra de Gúdar. From the Villarroya viewpoint we will have magnificent views of the town of Villarroya de los Pinares.

Fortanete, located at 1353 meters above sea level, is an Aragonese town and municipality, a beautiful town nestled in the Teruel Maestrazgo. Without a doubt, it is worth visiting (the tourist office is located in the Town Hall) the town, the Roman bridge, the bakery and the wood oven.
The origin of the town of Fortanete, as it is known today, dates back to the Middle Ages
